name: Jean-Pierre Rampal
abstract: Jean-Pierre Louis Rampal (7 January 1922 – 20 May 2000) was a French flautist and conductor. Rampal popularised the flute in the post–World War II years, recovering flute compositions from the Baroque era, and spurring contemporary composers, such as Francis Poulenc, to create new works that have become modern standards in the flautist's…

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Mabern
about_quotes.evidence: Hubert Laws, go out and do concerts with Jean Pierre Rampal played that stuff, and they couldn't play here.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Mabern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: you hear people like [James] Galway and Jean-Pierre Rampal, but Julius Baker was the guy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
